basic.q: Choose the correct pronoun to complete the sentence: '___ am a student.'
Identify the pronoun in the sentence: 'Mary gave him a book.'
Fill in the blank with the correct possessive pronoun: 'This is my pen. That pen is ___.'
Choose the correct subject pronoun: '___ went to the store together.'
Identify the pronoun type of the word 'myself' in: 'I made this cake myself.'
Complete the sentence with the correct demonstrative pronoun: '___ is a beautiful painting hanging on that wall.'
Choose the correct object pronoun: 'Please give the report to ___.'
Find the pronoun in the sentence: 'Everyone enjoyed the party.'
Fill in the blank: 'John and ___ are playing football.'
Choose the correct possessive adjective/determiner: 'Is this ___ car?'
Identify the pronoun: 'The dog hurt itself when it jumped.'
Complete the sentence: 'Sarah invited Mark and ___ to her birthday party.'
Choose the correct pronoun: '___ are my favorite shoes.'
Identify the pronoun in: 'Who left this door open?'
Fill in the blank: 'The teacher praised ___ for doing a great job.'
Choose the correct reflexive pronoun: 'The cat cleaned ___.'
Identify the pronoun type of 'someone' in: 'Someone is knocking at the door.'
Complete the sentence: 'Is that book ___ or mine?'
Choose the correct pronoun: 'Neither of the boys completed ___ homework.'
Find the pronoun in: 'What did you say?'
Fill in the blank: 'Give the toy back to ___.'
Choose the correct pronoun: 'My sister and ___ love reading mystery novels.'
Identify the pronoun: 'This is the house where I was born.'
Complete the sentence: 'The children baked the cookies all by ___.'
Choose the correct option: '___ of the options is correct.'
Identify the pronoun in: 'He is the man who helped me.'
Fill in the blank: 'Can you help ___ with this heavy box?'
Choose the correct pronoun: 'Everybody has ___ own opinion.'
Identify the relative pronoun in: 'The car that I bought is red.'
Complete the sentence: '___ are delicious apples.'

advanced.q: Choose the correct pronoun to complete the formal sentence: 'It was ___ who answered the phone during the emergency.'

Identify the case of the pronoun capitalized in: 'The committee awarded WHOMP with the prize.' (Correction variant: Whom did you invite?) -> Choose the correct case for 'Whom' when used as a direct object:
Fill in the blank with the correct relative pronoun: 'The scientist ___ research revolutionized physics won the Nobel Prize.'
Choose the correct pronoun form following a comparison: 'She is much taller than ___.'
Identify the antecedent ambiguity error or select the correct pronoun clarity sentence:
options: John told Mark that he needed to work harder.
The box contained a watch, which was broken.
Only sentence B has a completely clear relative clause.

advanced.q: Complete the sentence using the correct intensive pronoun for emphasis: 'The director ___ signed the contract.'
Choose the correct option for formal agreement: 'Each of the participants must submit ___ application by Friday.'
Identify the grammatical function of 'what' in: 'I know what you are thinking.'
options: Relative pronoun introducing a noun clause

advanced.q: Fill in the blank with the correct compound relative pronoun: 'You may invite ___ you like to the gala.'
Choose the correct pronoun case after a preposition: 'This matter is strictly between you and ___.'
Identify the indefinite pronoun that takes a singular verb: '___ of the candidates has arrived yet.'
Complete the sentence: 'Whose jacket is this? It is ___.'
Choose the correct pronoun to complete: 'Let ___ who are without sin cast the first stone.'
Identify the correct usage of 'who' vs 'whom' in: '___ did you see at the conference?'
Fill in the blank: 'The management team expressed ___ dissatisfaction with the quarterly report.'
Choose the correct correlative pronoun/adjective structure: '___ you sow, ___ shall you reap.'
Identify the type of pronoun underlined/capitalized in: 'WHATEVER happens, stay calm.'
Complete the sentence with standard formal grammar: 'It is important that every student bring ___ own laptop.'
Choose the correct pronoun choice for an elliptical clause: 'He can run as fast as ___.'
Identify the error or correct choice: 'Nominalizations and pronoun reference errors can obscure meaning. In 'John and David went out, but he was tired,' the pronoun reference is:'
Fill in the blank with the correct reciprocal pronoun: 'The two rival companies blamed ___ for the market crash.'
Choose the correct option: '___ intelligent a student she is, she still needs to study.'
Identify the pronoun acting as a subject complement in: 'The winner of the competition will be ___.'
Complete the sentence: 'Give the award to ___ deserves it most.'
Choose the correct grammatical pronoun form: 'The honor belongs to ___ and me.'
Identify the function of 'that' in: 'The book THAT is on the desk belongs to the professor.'
options: Relative pronoun introducing a restrictive clause

advanced.q: Fill in the blank: 'Hardly anyone expressed ___ approval of the new policy.'
Choose the correct option: 'Between you and ___, I think the plan will fail.'
Identify the pronoun type in: 'SUCH is the nature of politics.'
Complete the sentence: 'If anyone calls, tell ___ that I am in a meeting.'
